game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Coffee Stack:** Players control a stack of coffee cups that automatically moves forward along a linear path, using horizontal swipes (left/right) to navigate between 3-5 lanes. The core mechanic involves collecting empty cups by running over them, adding to the stack, and then passing through various gates that apply modifications like filling with coffee, adding sleeves, or attaching lids, each step increasing the cup's value. Obstacles, such as barriers or grinders, reduce the stack size by a fixed number of cups (e.g., -1 cup per collision), requiring skillful lane changes to maintain a large stack. At the end of each level, players serve the accumulated, customized coffee cups to a line of customers, earning coins based on the number and quality of cups delivered (e.g., a fully customized cup earns 10 coins, an empty cup earns 1 coin). Coins collected are used in a meta-game to unlock new coffee recipes, upgrade shop aesthetics, or purchase permanent bonuses like starting with 2 cups or increasing base cup value by 10%, with difficulty scaling through longer paths, more complex gate sequences, and increased obstacle density.
game_info.description: Navigate your growing stack of coffee cups through a lane-based course! Collect empty cups, pass through gates to fill them with coffee, add sleeves, and attach lids to increase their value. Avoid obstacles that destroy cups from your stack. At the end, serve your customized cups to customers for coins. Earn more coins with larger, fully-customized stacks!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left / A: Move stack to the left lane
Arrow Right / D: Move stack to the right lane
Enter: Start the game from the start screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause the game
R: Restart from game over screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic lane movement and cup collection mechanics. The test verifies that the player can switch between lanes and collect empty cups to grow the stack. This ensures the fundamental gameplay loop works correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the stack left and right between lanes while moving forward. Collect empty cups placed in different lanes. Avoid some obstacles intentionally to test collision detection. Continue until reaching the end or losing all cups.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The stack should successfully move between lanes, collect cups that increase stack size, and lose cups when hitting obstacles. The game should transition to the serving phase when reaching the end. Test passes if lane switching and cup collection work smoothly without errors.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing game over condition when all cups are lost. This verifies that the game properly handles losing all cups to obstacles and transitions to the appropriate game over state.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Start the game and intentionally collide with multiple obstacles to lose all cups from the stack. Continue until the stack is completely empty, triggering the game over condition.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all cups are lost. A game over screen should display with the option to restart. Test passes if the game handles empty stack correctly without crashing.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing the pause functionality during gameplay. This ensures the ESC key properly pauses and unpauses the game without affecting game state.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Start playing, collect some cups, then press ESC to pause. Wait a moment, then press ESC again to unpause. Continue playing to verify the game state is preserved correctly.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The game should freeze all movement when paused and display a "PAUSED" overlay. Unpausing should resume exactly where it left off. Test passes if pause/unpause works without state corruption.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing multiple gate sequences to verify customization stacking. This ensures that cups can receive multiple upgrades in sequence and that the visual representation updates correctly.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Collect cups and then navigate through multiple gates in a specific order: first coffee gate, then sleeve gate, then lid gate. Observe the cup appearance changes after each gate.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Cups should show cumulative customization as they pass through gates. A cup should show coffee fill, then add a sleeve band, then add a lid on top. Test passes if visual customization stacks properly and is reflected in the final coin value.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing edge case of collecting cups after passing through customization gates. This verifies that newly collected cups don't inherit customizations and that the stack maintains separate states for different cups.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Start with a few cups, pass through a coffee gate to customize them, then collect new empty cups afterward. Continue to observe if new cups are empty or inherit customization.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Only cups that physically pass through gates should be customized. New cups collected after gates should be empty. Test passes if customization is applied correctly on a per-cup basis.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing the serving phase and coin calculation. This verifies that the final scoring system correctly counts cups and applies value multipliers based on customization level.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Create a stack with mixed customization levels: some empty cups, some with just coffee, some fully customized. Reach the end and observe the coin calculation and serving animation.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Each cup should be served individually with appropriate coin values: 1 for empty, progressively more for customized cups up to 10 for fully customized. The total score should accurately reflect the stack composition. Test passes if scoring is accurate and visible.
